PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael D. Devin appearing for Ting Hua Tam aka Tina T. Tam (Petitioner). Christopher Abrams appearing by CourtCall for Wendy Tam and Elaine Tam (Personal Representatives).

The Court noted that a spousal property petition is not a proper form to approve this settlement agreement and stated that it can make a determination that 50% of RBC is community property. The Third Amended Petition for Spousal Property is granted in part and denied in part. Counsel is to prepare a revised order and submit it to the Court for review and signature. The petition is denied in all other respects without prejudice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Aug-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Devin for Ting Tam (Petitioner).
Christopher Abrams appeared by telephone for Wendy Tam and Elaine Tam.

Mr. Abrams stated they have come to an agreement. The Court stated that there are notice issues and that notice went directly to the Attorney, but not to the Executors. Mr. Abrams stated that he is willing to waive notice on behalf of his Clients. Mr. Devin stated that he believes they have an agreement as to a stipulated order that would allow Mr. Abram's Clients to deal with what ever they need and transfer a 50% interest to his Client. The Court noted that the amended petition seems to refer to earlier pleadings that are not attached, stated that three different assets are addressed which each raise different issues, and that it appears that the agreement has to do with the RBC account. Mr. Devin responded yes, stated that his Client signed a quit claim deed regarding 1580 Pacific (upper pacific) and that it is not necessary for the Beach Road property to be administered here. Mr. Abrams stated that the pacific property is titled in the name of the trust. The Court questions how the spousal property petition gets the RBC account to the Decedent's Son. Mr. Devin stated that it is his understanding that Mr. Tam used community assets to fund that account and his Client has an interest, that he is trying to get her 50% interest to her, that he can file something showing what the assets are and that Francis is an adult. Mr. Abrams stated that perhaps the best way to go is to file a fourth amended petition listing only the RBC account and that addresses the Court's concerns. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to August 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that it is expecting a timely properly noticed amended petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Continued To May-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Devin for Ting Hua Tam (Petitioner).
Christopher Abrahams for Wendy Tam and Elaine Tam (Petitioners/Executors).

The Court informed the parties that the petition is not ready procedurally, that it does not have a response to the Probate Examiner's notes, that it does not have attachment 7 which is different from attachment 7b and that it does not have a proposed order. Mr. Devin stated that he thinks they may be in agreement as to some of the property, suggested that they discuss the matter and come back in two weeks, and stated that he will speak with his Client about responding to the Examiner's notes. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to May 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
